diagrams.title: Decibel Scale with Common Sounds
diagrams.description: Shows noise levels from whisper (30 dB) to jet engine (150 dB) with hearing damage threshold marked at 85 dB
diagrams.purpose: Understand relative loudness and identify hazardous noise levels

diagrams.title: Noise Control Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Three-tier approach showing source, path, and receiver control measures in order of effectiveness
elements: Level 1: Source Control (most effective) - Equipment design, maintenance, substitution
Level 2: Path Control (moderate) - Barriers, distance, isolation, absorption
Level 3: Receiver Control (last resort) - PPE, building insulation, time restriction
diagrams.purpose: Learn priority approaches to noise management and control

diagrams.title: Ear Anatomy and Noise Damage
diagrams.description: Cross-section of human ear showing how sound waves travel and damage hair cells in cochlea
elements: Inner ear: Cochlea with hair cells (damaged by loud noise)
Auditory nerve: Transmits signals to brain
Hair cell damage: Permanent hearing loss
diagrams.purpose: Understand the physiological mechanism of noise-induced hearing loss
